message.content:
I can’t browse the web in real time, so I don’t have the very latest OpenAI headlines. I can help you quickly get up to speed in a few ways:

What I can do
- If you share a few current headlines or links, I’ll summarize them.
- I can point you to reliable sources and explain what to look for in OpenAI news (product updates, partnerships, safety/regulatory issues, pricing/API changes, leadership moves).
- I can give you a quick guide on how to monitor OpenAI news yourself (recommended sources and how to set up alerts).

Where to look for the latest
- OpenAI official blog and press page
- OpenAI’s social channels (X/Twitter, LinkedIn)
- Major tech outlets: TechCrunch, The Verge, Reuters, Bloomberg, CNBC, The Information
- Business/legal coverage: Financial Times, The Wall Street Journal, Bloomberg Law
- General news aggregators: Google News / Apple News with “OpenAI” keyword

If you want, I can also prepare a short, current-news brief for you once you paste the headlines you’re seeing, or I can guide you step-by-step on setting up a quick alert system. What would you prefer?
